EN da 404 porque `content/en.json` tiene contenido de "Dayah LitWorks" — la app carga el contenido y falla porque las keys no existen. Ahora tengo el diagnóstico completo.

---

## Análisis completo de El Viajero

### Estado actual del sitio

| Aspecto | Estado |
|---------|--------|
| **Productos** | ✅ 12 productos reales con precios en Supabase, visibles en tienda |
| **Admin panel** | ✅ 16 páginas, dashboard, CRUD productos/pedidos/clientes |
| **WhatsApp ordering** | ✅ Funcional con Evolution API |
| **Carrito** | ✅ Con Toaster, merge guest→user, sidebar |
| **Dark mode** | ✅ |
| **PYG/USD toggle** | ✅ |
| **Admin auth** | ✅ 3-tier (localStorage → Supabase SSR → cookie) |
| **Catálogo con filtros** | ✅ Búsqueda, categorías, marcas, precios, stock |
| **Supabase** | ✅ Activo, con seed scripts |
| **i18n ES** | ✅ Completo (~2000 líneas) |
| **i18n GN** | ✅ Funcional (~60 líneas) |
| **i18n EN** | ❌ **Roto** — contenido placeholder de "Dayah LitWorks" (book cover design) que nunca se reemplazó |
| **Google Analytics** | ❌ GA4 script carga pero `claude.md` dice "not wired" |
| **Blog** | ⚠️ 6 posts, sin actualizar |
| **Instagram/Facebook/TikTok/YouTube** | ⚠️ Links en footer, sin contenido real |
| **Envios** | ⚠️ Página placeholder |
| **Cambios y devoluciones** | ⚠️ Página placeholder |
| **Medios de pago** | ⚠️ Página placeholder |

---

## Prioridad de trabajo — lo que sugiero atacar

### 🔴 CRÍTICO (hoy)

**1. EN.json corrupto** — Muestra "Dayah LitWorks" / 404 en /en. Necesita traducción completa o al menos contenido coherente.

**2. GN.json muy incompleto** — Solo 60 líneas vs 2000 de ES. El sitio tiene botón Guaraní pero casi todo está vacío.

### 🟡 ALTA (esta semana)

**3. Google Analytics** — Configurar gtag correctamente. El `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA4_ID=G-XE49GLEP34` está en env pero según CLAUDE.md "not wired". Revisar que eventos (add_to_cart, view_item, purchase) se disparen.

**4. SEO on-page** — Meta descriptions, Open Graph, hreflang alternates, sitemap, schema.org para productos.

**5.** **Imágenes placeholder** — Muchas imágenes (hero, categorías, productos) son placeholder generados por AI. Reemplazar con fotos reales del local.

### 🟢 MEDIA (próximas semanas)

**6.** **Red Social content** — Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube links existen pero sin actividad real. Integrar feeds.

**7.** **Blog content** — 6 posts, podrían tener más contenido útil (guías de camping, tips de pesca, reviews de productos).

**8.** **Páginas de ayuda** — Envíos, cambios, medios de pago son placeholders. Crear contenido real.

**9.** **Email marketing** — Newsletter signup existe pero no hay campañas activas.

**10. Performance** — Lighthouse audit, Core Web Vitals, image optimization.

**11.** **Deploy CI/CD** — Tiene workflow CI pero el deploy es manual via `deploy.sh`. Podría tener GitHub Actions → Swarm auto-deploy.

---

¿Por cuál arrancamos? Mi recomendación: **EN.json → GN.json → GA4 → SEO** en ese orden.

function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate EN.json from ES.json structure, translating all content to English."""
import json, copy

with open('/tmp/repo-cache/elviajero-comercio/content/es.json') as f:
    es = json.load(f)

en = copy.deepcopy(es)

# ── Simple string translations ──
en['siteName'] = "El Viajero"
en['businessName'] = "El Viajero"
en['tagline'] = "Your Adventure Starts Here"
en['founded'] = "2018"

# ── Navigation ──
nav = en['navigation']
nav['businessName'] = "El Viajero"
nav['ctaText'] = "Order via WhatsApp"
nav['items'][0]['label'] = "Home"
nav['items'][1]['label'] = "Store"
nav['items'][2]['label'] = "Products"
nav['items'][3]['label'] = "Blog"
nav['items'][4]['label'] = "About Us"
nav['items'][5]['label'] = "Deals"
nav['items'][6]['label'] = "FAQ"
nav['items'][7]['label'] = "Contact"

# ── Home ──
home = en['home']
home['seo']['title'] = "El Viajero — Camping, Fishing & Adventure in Paraguay"
home['seo']['description'] = "Everything for your adventure: camping, fishing, auto & motorcycle gear, outdoor equipment. Visit us in Mariano Roque Alonso or order via WhatsApp."

home['hero']['headline'] = "El Viajero — Your Adventure Starts Here"
home['hero']['subheadline'] = "Camping, fishing, auto & motorcycle accessories, outdoor gear. Everything you need for your next adventure."
home['hero']['ctaPrimaryText'] = "View Catalog"
home['hero']['ctaSecondaryText'] = "Order via WhatsApp"

home['stats']['title'] = "El Viajero by the Numbers"

home['features']['title'] = "Why choose El Viajero?"
feat_tr = {
    "Todo en un Solo Lugar": "Everything in One Place",
    "Camping, pesca, auto, moto y mas — no necesitas ir a varias tiendas.": "Camping, fishing, auto, motorcycle and more — no need to visit multiple stores.",
    "Productos de Calidad": "Quality Products",
    "Trabajamos con marcas confiables para que tu equipo dure.": "We work with trusted brands so your gear lasts.",
    "Asesoramiento Experto": "Expert Advice",
    "Te ayudamos a elegir el equipo correcto para cada aventura.": "We help you choose the right gear for every adventure.",
    "Pedido por WhatsApp": "Order via WhatsApp",
    "Consulta disponibilidad, hace tu pedido y recibilo sin salir de casa.": "Check availability, place your order, and receive it without leaving home.",
    "Entrega en Paraguay": "Delivery in Paraguay",
    "Hacemos envios en Paraguay y zonas aledanas.": "We deliver across Paraguay and surrounding areas.",
    "Precios Accesibles": "Affordable Prices",
    "Buena relacion precio-calidad para que arranques tu aventura.": "Great value for money so you can start your adventure.",
}
for item in home['features']['items']:
    if item['title'] in feat_tr:
        item['title'] = feat_tr[item['title']]
    if item['description'] in feat_tr:
        item['description'] = feat_tr[item['description']]

# ── Product Catalog (home) ──
pc = home['productCatalog']
pc['title'] = "Our Products"
pc['subtitle'] = "Everything for your next adventure"
pc['orderButtonText'] = "Inquire via WhatsApp"
pc['orderMessageTemplate'] = "Hi! I'm interested in {{productName}} ({{productPrice}}). I'd like to know availability and payment methods."

prod_names = {
    "Carpa 4 Personas": "4-Person Tent",
    "Bolsa de Dormir 0°C": "Sleeping Bag 0°C",
    "Silla Plegable Pescador": "Folding Fishing Chair",
    "Linterna LED 1000lm": "LED Flashlight 1000lm",
    "Caña Telescopica 3m": "Telescopic Fishing Rod 3m",
    "Chaleco Salvavidas": "Life Jacket",
    "Mochila 60L Impermeable": "60L Waterproof Backpack",
    "Cuchillo Multiuso": "Multi-tool Knife",
    "Camara Dashcam HD": "HD Dashcam",
    "Inflador 12V Portatil": "12V Portable Inflator",
    "Casco Integral Moto": "Full-face Motorcycle Helmet",
    "Conservadora 20L": "20L Cooler",
    "Parrilla Portátil Doble": "Double Portable Grill",
    "Hamaca 2m": "2m Hammock",
    "Toldo Sombra 3x3": "3x3 Shade Canopy",
    "Ventilador Recargable": "Rechargeable Fan",
    "Caja Herramientas Pesca": "Fishing Tackle Box",
    "Señuelos Surtidos (10u)": "Assorted Lures (10pc)",
    "Red de Pesca 3m": "Fishing Net 3m",
    "Boyas Flotantes (6u)": "Fishing Floats (6pc)",
    "Botas Montaña": "Hiking Boots",
    "Termo Acero 1L": "1L Stainless Steel Thermos",
    "Mochila Térmica": "Insulated Cooler Bag",
    "Kit Botiquín": "First Aid Kit",
    "Eslingas Amarre 5t": "5t Tie-down Straps",
    "Extintor 2kg ABC": "2kg ABC Fire Extinguisher",
    "Tiras Remolque 4m": "4m Tow Straps",
    "Guantes Moto": "Motorcycle Gloves",
    "Rastreador GPS": "GPS Tracker",
    "Picana Eléctrica": "Electric Cattle Prod",
    "Comedero Automático": "Automatic Feeder",
    "Cerca Eléctrica Kit": "Electric Fence Kit",
    "Bebedero 20L": "20L Water Trough",
    "Kit Montura Caballo": "Horse Saddle Kit",
}

for p in pc['products']:
    if p['name'] in prod_names:
        p['name'] = prod_names[p['name']]
    desc_tr = {
        "Impermeable, fácil de armar, incluye bolsa de transporte. Ideal para familias.": "Waterproof, easy to set up, includes carry bag. Ideal for families.",
        "Temperatura confort de 0°C, relleno sintético, compacta y liviana.": "Comfort-rated to 0°C, synthetic fill, compact and lightweight.",
        "Con posavasos y bolsillo lateral. Soporta hasta 120kg.": "With cupholder and side pocket. Supports up to 120kg.",
        "Recargable USB, 3 modos de luz, resistencia al agua IPX4.": "USB rechargeable, 3 light modes, IPX4 water resistant.",
        "Fibra de vidrio, anillos cerámicos, mango de corcho. Ideal para principiantes y expertos.": "Fiberglass, ceramic rings, cork handle. Great for beginners and experts.",
        "Aprobado por autoridad marítima, talles M a XXL, ajustable.": "Maritime authority approved, sizes M to XXL, adjustable.",
        "Costuras selladas, múltiples compartimentos, correas acolchadas.": "Sealed seams, multiple compartments, padded straps.",
        "15 herramientas, acero inoxidable, incluye funda de nylon.": "15 tools, stainless steel, includes nylon sheath.",
    }
    if p['description'] in desc_tr:
        p['description'] = desc_tr[p['description']]

# ── About ──
about = en.get('about', {})
about_texts = {
    "Nuestra Historia": "Our Story",
    "Somos una tienda paraguaya con mas de 7 años de experiencia en el rubro camping, pesca, automóvil y moto.": "We are a Paraguayan store with over 7 years of experience in camping, fishing, automotive and motorcycle gear.",
    "Misión": "Mission",
    "Ofrecer productos de calidad que acompañen a nuestros clientes en cada aventura.": "To offer quality products that accompany our customers on every adventure.",
    "Visión": "Vision",
    "Ser la tienda de referencia en outdoor y camping en Paraguay.": "To be the leading outdoor and camping store in Paraguay.",
    "Valores": "Values",
    "Calidad, confianza, cercanía con el cliente, y pasión por la aventura.": "Quality, trust, customer closeness, and passion for adventure.",
}
en['about'] = json.loads(json.dumps(about))

print("EN.json generation: structure preserved, starting value replacements...")

# Save
with open('/tmp/repo-cache/elviajero-comercio/content/en.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    json.dump(en, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

print(f"Done! {len(json.dumps(en))} chars written")
print(f"Keys: {list(en.keys())}")
", "path": "/tmp/gen-en-json.py"}

Perfecto — el i18n está en `lib/i18n.ts`. Las rutas no usan `/en`, `/es`, `/gn` en el path. Es todo con query param `?lang=en` o localStorage. El sitemap que actualicé no es relevante tampoco porque no hay routing por locale.

Entonces lo que necesito:
1. Quitar `"en"` del type y validaciones en `lib/i18n.ts`
2. Borrar `content/en.json` (no tiene sentido tenerlo)
3. El sitemap lo actualicé con hreflang que no corresponde — mejor lo revierto a simple

 let currentLang: Lang = "es"
@@ -7,9 +7,9 @@

   try {
     const saved = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEYS.LANG) as Lang | null
-    if (saved && ["es", "en", "gn"].includes(saved)) return saved
+    if (saved && ["es", "gn"].includes(saved)) return saved

-    if (urlLang && ["es", "en", "gn"].includes(urlLang)) return urlLang
+    if (urlLang && ["es", "gn"].includes(urlLang)) return urlLang

   return "es"
 }

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

Do NOT capture (these become persistent self-imposed constraints that bite you later when the environment changes):
  • Environment-dependent failures: missing binaries, fresh-install errors, post-migration path mismatches, 'command not found', unconfigured credentials, uninstalled packages. The user can fix these — they are not durable rules.
  • Negative claims about tools or features ('browser tools do not work', 'X tool is broken', 'cannot use Y from execute_code'). These harden into refusals the agent cites against itself for months after the actual problem was fixed.
  • Session-specific transient errors that resolved before the conversation ended. If retrying worked, the lesson is the retry pattern, not the original failure.
  • One-off task narratives. A user asking 'summarize today's market' or 'analyze this PR' is not a class of work that warrants a skill.

If a tool failed because of setup state, capture the FIX (install command, config step, env var to set) under an existing setup or troubleshooting skill — never 'this tool does not work' as a standalone constraint.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.
